questions.label: How much maintenance do you want?

options.label: I am curious about carbon steel

rules.title: VG10 stainless-clad nakiri, 165mm
rules.why: A nakiri gives you a flat, confident vegetable profile. VG10 stainless-clad or simple stainless keeps rust anxiety low while still taking a crisp edge.
rules.avoid: Do not use it for bones, twisting through squash, frozen food or pretending it is a cleaver. Rectangle does not mean crowbar.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: Shapton Kuromaku/Pro 1000 for fast splash-and-go sharpening, or King 1000/6000 if budget and feedback matter more than speed.

maintenanceKit.label: Strop / deburring
maintenanceKit.text: Leather strop or deburring block after stone work. Use it to clean the apex, not to avoid making one.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: Hasegawa or Asahi if you want a synthetic edge-friendly board; hinoki if you accept more care.

maintenanceKit.label: Storage
maintenanceKit.text: Blade guard or saya. Nakiri edges are thin and drawer chaos is undefeated.

rules.title: Stainless gyuto or western chef knife, 180-210mm
rules.steel: VG10, AUS-8, X50CrMoV15 or other stainless
rules.why: This is the practical lane: enough length for onions, protein and herbs, without carbon-steel homework. Tojiro DP, MAC, Global, Victorinox and German classics all live somewhere here.
rules.avoid: Skip very thick bolsters if you want easier sharpening, and skip very thin lasers if your board technique is still enthusiastic.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: Shapton 1000 for a fast one-stone routine; King if you want lower cost and more soaking-stone feel.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: A large soft board matters more than another handle material debate.

maintenanceKit.label: Storage
maintenanceKit.text: Use a guard, strip or roll. Edges and roommates both behave better with boundaries.

rules.title: Stainless santoku or nakiri, 165-180mm
rules.why: For mixed home cooking, a santoku gives more tip versatility while a nakiri makes vegetable prep cleaner and more fun.
rules.avoid: Avoid buying purely by Damascus pattern. The board, grind and heat treatment will matter after the Instagram moment expires.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: A clean 1000 grit edge is enough for most vegetables; add 3000 only when you can deburr reliably.

maintenanceKit.label: Strop
maintenanceKit.text: Strop lightly. Heavy stropping rounds edges and creates confidence without cutting performance.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: Hasegawa, Asahi or hinoki depending on care tolerance.

rules.title: Carbon or semi-stainless Sanjo gyuto, 210mm
rules.why: A Yoshikane, Wakui or Toyama-style lane gives you food release, grind character and the joy of wiping your knife like a responsible adult.
rules.avoid: Skip this if you will leave lemon juice on the edge or store it wet. Carbon steel is not haunted; it is just reactive.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: 1000 grit for edge reset, 3000-6000 only after you control pressure and deburring.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: Use an edge-friendly board. Hard glass, bamboo and marble are knife sadness with a countertop.

maintenanceKit.label: Storage
maintenanceKit.text: Dry fully before the guard. Carbon plus damp saya equals orange regret.

rules.title: Stainless petty, 120-150mm
rules.why: A petty is the quiet knife that does citrus, herbs, garlic, trimming and board-adjacent work without making you unsheathe a 240mm personality test.
rules.avoid: Do not use it as a paring knife in the air unless the handle and control feel right.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: Same 1000 stone as your main knife. Do not buy a tiny stone for a tiny knife.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: Small knives still deserve a sane board.

rules.title: Sujihiki or western slicer, length only if your board allows it
rules.steel: Stainless or stainless-clad for low fuss
rules.why: Long, clean pulls beat sawing. Buy the length your board and kitchen can actually support.
rules.avoid: Skip if you mainly dice onions. A slicer is not a personality replacement for an all-purpose knife.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: A clean 1000 edge matters; polishing higher is optional and food-dependent.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: Use a board long enough for full strokes.

maintenanceKit.label: Storage
maintenanceKit.text: Long blades need sleeves or a proper roll.

rules.title: One real stone, one strop, one boring routine
rules.why: Start with a 1000 grit stone, learn burr formation and removal, then add refinement. Buying six stones before you can deburr is how drawers become geology museums.
rules.avoid: Avoid mystery angle gadgets if they stop you learning pressure, contact and burr behavior.

maintenanceKit.label: Stone
maintenanceKit.text: Shapton 1000 for splash-and-go speed; King 1000/6000 for budget and feedback.

maintenanceKit.label: Strop
maintenanceKit.text: Use lightly after burr removal. Strop as punctuation, not as the sentence.

maintenanceKit.label: Board
maintenanceKit.text: A good board extends the time between sharpening sessions.
